refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) history log captures all updates with reason codes and approval chain; (d) traceability links each target to originating mission parameter; (e) safety margins are explicitly derived and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record baseline performance requirements with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish traceability links between each performance target and originating worksite input.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Derive and document explicit safety margins for all safety-critical performance parameters.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirement engine (rules-based or ML-driven) that ingests worksite mission parameters, computes targets, and logs all changes to a versioned requirement database with full audit trail.
DP-B: Distributed performance derivation across vehicle and teleoperation modules, with each module computing its own targets and synchronizing via a shared requirement registry.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by Site Manager with optional auto-suggestion from mission context; all changes logged in a simple change log.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance requirement engine with rules-based derivation logic, versioned requirement database, and comprehensive audit trail. The engine ingests worksite mission context (terrain classification, payload mass, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA) via Tasking interface, applies deterministic or calibrated rules to derive performance targets, records baseline with metadata, and maintains full history of updates with reason codes and approval chain.

potential_sources: Coupling to Tasking interface (mission context input) is necessary and well-defined.
Coupling to SafetyAlert interface (safety margin derivation) is necessary and well-defined.
Coupling to Telemetry interface (actual performance feedback for calibration) is necessary and well-defined.
No unnecessary cross-module dependencies; centralized engine minimizes diffusion.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling complexity. The engine is a single logical entity with clear input/output contracts. Decoupling from vehicle and teleoperation specifics is achieved by parameterizing rules rather than embedding them in those modules.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface at system initialization and update events.
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation rules are stable and can be captured in a rules engine or calibrated model.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived targets may be invalid; mitigation: validation rules and operator override capability.
RISK: Audit trail storage may grow large over system lifetime; mitigation: archival and compression strategies.
RISK: Safety margin derivation requires domain expertise; mitigation: involve Safety Manager in rule definition and validation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend explicit specification of: (1) rule set for deriving each performance target from mission parameters; (2) audit trail schema and retention policy; (3) operator override and approval workflow; (4) integration points with Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry interfaces.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the centralized performance requirement engine, including derivation logic, baseline recording, history management, and audit trail. This module is the system-level integrator and is responsible for orchestrating performance requirement derivation across all worksite mission contexts and maintaining the single source of truth for performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA, safety constraints) from Site Manager or automated mission planner. Used to trigger performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends derived safety margins and safety-critical performance targets to Safety Services for validation and approval. Receives safety constraints and approval status to incorporate into baseline.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives actual performance data (cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for calibration and validation of derived targets.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for distributed access to performance requirement baseline and history by Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, and Maintenance personnel.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator and natural owner of cross-cutting performance requirement management. Centralized engine minimizes diffusion. High containment score reflects that this is a system-level concern, not a vehicle or teleoperation concern.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing missions and reporting telemetry, not for deriving system-level performance requirements. Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would scatter requirement derivation logic and create tight coupling to vehicle-specific parameters, violating separation of concerns.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote control and monitoring, not for deriving system-level performance requirements. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would create unnecessary coupling and diffusion of requirement management logic.

Safety Services is responsible for safety validation and alert generation, not for deriving performance requirements. While Safety Services will validate and approve safety margins, the derivation engine itself belongs in NGHE Solution. Safety Services interfaces via SafetyAlert.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct owner because: (1) performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern that spans all mission contexts and operational modes; (2) centralized ownership ensures single source of truth and prevents diffusion of requirement logic across vehicle and teleoperation modules; (3) NGHE Solution already owns system integration and is the natural place for cross-cutting concerns; (4) clear interface contracts (Tasking in, SafetyAlert inout, Telemetry in, Network Link inout) enable clean separation from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module,' it may accumulate unrelated responsibilities. MITIGATION: Enforce strict interface contracts and regularly audit module scope.

RISK: Performance requirement derivation may require vehicle-specific or teleoperation-specific parameters not available at NGHE Solution level. MITIGATION: Parameterize rules and allow Vehicle/Teleoperation to provide context via Tasking interface.

RISK: Safety margin derivation may require tight coupling to Safety Services. MITIGATION: Use SafetyAlert interface for bidirectional communication; Safety Services validates and approves, NGHE Solution records.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) define explicit interface contracts for Tasking (mission context schema), SafetyAlert (safety margin approval workflow), Telemetry (performance feedback schema), and Network Link (access control and audit logging); (2) establish governance for performance derivation rules (who defines, who approves, change control); (3) design audit trail schema to support regulatory compliance and traceability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what must be done (derive, record, manage performance requirements with traceability and history). Selected DP-A (centralized performance requirement engine with rules-based derivation, versioned database, and audit trail) directly addresses all FR elements. Mapping is one-to-one and non-redundant. DP-A is implementable and testable.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: Centralized engine in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion. No duplication of requirement derivation logic across modules.
LOW RISK: Clear interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) prevent hidden dependencies.

MEDIUM RISK: If Vehicle or Teleoperation modules need to compute local performance targets (e.g., for real-time control), they may duplicate logic. MITIGATION: Provide API from NGHE Solution for local queries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container. Containment score of 95 reflects that performance requirement management is a system-level concern. Diffusion score of 5 reflects minimal risk of logic scattering. Interface contracts are clear and well-defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH: Verification is straightforward. (1) Functional verification: derive targets from known mission contexts and confirm correctness against expected values. (2) Traceability verification: confirm each target is linked to originating mission parameter. (3) History verification: confirm all updates are logged with metadata and audit trail is complete. (4) Safety verification: confirm safety margins are derived and approved by Safety Manager.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR: FR CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution via DP-A. Sub-FRs are allocated as follows: CR-NGHE-005.1 (derive targets) -> DP-A derivation engine; CR-NGHE-005.2 (record baseline) -> DP-A database; CR-NGHE-005.3 (maintain history) -> DP-A audit trail; CR-NGHE-005.4 (traceability) -> DP-A metadata schema; CR-NGHE-005.5 (safety margins) -> DP-A + SafetyAlert interface. All allocations are clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Specify detailed interface contracts for Tasking (mission context schema), SafetyAlert (safety margin approval workflow), Telemetry (performance feedback schema), and Network Link (access control).
Define performance derivation rules and rule change control process. Involve Safety Manager and Site Manager in rule definition.
Specify audit trail schema, retention policy, and archival strategy.
Define operator override and approval workflow for performance requirement updates.
Establish acceptance criteria for performance requirement derivation accuracy (e.g., derived targets must match actual performance within X%).

decision.justification: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 is well-defined and decomposable into five atomic sub-FRs. DP-A (centralized performance requirement engine) is sound and addresses all FR elements. IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct with high containment score and low diffusion risk. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. However, the five sub-FRs are not yet atomic and require further decomposition to enable detailed design and implementation. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record baseline performance requirements with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish traceability links between each performance target and originating worksite input.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Derive and document explicit safety margins for all safety-critical performance parameters.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface at system initialization and update events.
Performance derivation rules are stable and can be captured in a rules engine or calibrated model.
Safety margin derivation rules are defined by Safety Manager and approved before deployment.
Audit trail storage is managed with archival and compression strategies to prevent unbounded growth.
NGHE Solution has sufficient computational capacity to derive targets in real-time or near-real-time.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived targets may be invalid. Mitigation: validation rules and operator override capability.

If performance derivation rules are incorrect or incomplete, targets may not reflect actual mission requirements. Mitigation: involve Site Manager and Safety Manager in rule definition and validation.
If audit trail is not properly secured, it may be tampered with or lost. Mitigation: implement access control and backup/recovery strategies.
If Vehicle or Teleoperation modules need local performance targets, they may duplicate logic. Mitigation: provide API from NGHE Solution for local queries.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of performance derivation rules for each target type (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
Audit trail schema and retention policy.
Interface contracts for Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, and Network Link.
Acceptance criteria for performance requirement derivation accuracy.
Safety Manager approval of safety margin derivation rules.
Site Manager input on mission context parameters and expected performance targets.
